since_last: 38% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +2.76%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 38% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +0.28% · BTC -2.31% · Gold +1.47% · Nasdaq100 +1.11%. 38% of 87 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +2.76%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +2.76%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -2.77%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +16.78%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +16.78%, AMAT +11.05%, TER +9.10%, KLAC +8.05%, QCOM +6.82%. Laggards: INOD -10.79%, DELL -5.58%, AI -5.15%, PLTR -5.14%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -0.86% at $153.21
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -0.86% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
